Feature_Name: Inter gNB DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup Procedure

3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN; Architecture description, NR; Radio Resource Control (RRC); Protocol specification

Feature_Description: The UE Context Setup procedure is used to establish the UE Context, including SRB, DRB, BH RLC channel, Uu Relay RLC channel, PC5 Relay RLC channel, and SL DRB configuration.
This specific procedure facilitates Inter-gNB-DU LTM (L1/L2 Triggered Mobility) Handover, enabling the UE to move from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation.
It involves the gNB-CU preparing and sending an F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST" message to a candidate gNB-DU, which then responds with an F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE" message.
Feature_Objectives: To establish a UE Context in a candidate gNB-DU for an Inter-gNB-DU LTM Handover.
To configure LTM-specific parameters, including CSI resource configuration and lower layer RRC configurations, in the candidate gNB-DU.
To facilitate early synchronization (early TA acquisition) for the LTM candidate cell.
To establish a UE-associated logical F1-connection if one does not already exist.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 measurement reports from the UE.
The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell.

The UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message includes the candidate cell ID, CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM, and may include the LTM configuration ID mapping list, a request for PRACH resources, and/or lower layer configuration for generating the reference configuration.
If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the LTM configuration request, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message includes the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
If no UE-associated logical F1-connection exists, it shall be established as part of this procedure.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU initiates the procedure by sending a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU.

2. The UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message contains LTM-specific information such as `LTM Information Setup IE` (including `LTM Indicator IE`, `SpCell ID IE`, `Reference Configuration IE`, `CSI Resource Configuration IE`), `LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE`, and `Early Sync Information Request IE`.
3. If the gNB-DU succeeds in establishing the UE context and accepting the LTM configuration, it replies to the gNB-CU with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.

4. The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message includes LTM-specific information such as `Requested Target Cell ID IE`, `LTM Configuration IE` (including `Reference Configuration Information IE`, `Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE`), `Early Sync Information IE`, `Early UL Sync Configuration IE`, and `Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE`.
5. If no UE-associated logical F1-connection exists, it shall be established as part of this procedure.
6. The gNB-CU shall consider the `LTM Configuration IE` in the response as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell.
7. The gNB-CU shall consider the `Early UL Sync Configuration IE` and `Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE` as the generated early UL sync information from the accepted candidate cell.
8. If the `Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE` is set to "complete", the gNB-CU shall consider the LTM candidate configuration as a complete candidate configuration.
1. If the gNB-DU is not able to establish an F1 UE context, or cannot even establish one bearer, it shall consider the procedure as failed and reply with the UE CONTEXT SETUP FAILURE message.

2. If the `LTM Indicator IE` was included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall include the received `SpCell ID IE` as the `Requested Target Cell ID IE` in the UE CONTEXT SETUP FAILURE message.
3. If the gNB-DU is not able to accept the `SpCell ID IE` in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, it shall reply with the UE CONTEXT SETUP FAILURE message with an appropriate cause value.

1. If the `LTM Indicator IE` was included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, but the candidate cell in the `SpCell ID IE` was not prepared using the same UE-associated signaling connection, the gNB-DU shall ignore this candidate cell.

Information_Elements.IE_Definition:
LTMConfiguration ::= SEQUENCE {
 sSBInformation     SSBInformation,
 referenceConfigurationInformation  ReferenceConfigurationInformation   OPTIONAL,
 completeCandidateConfigurationIndicator  CompleteCandidateConfigurationIndicator     OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfig    LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfigSUL   LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions      ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMConfiguration-ExtIEs } } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: RRC `CellGroupConfig IE` is piggy-backed within F1AP messages to convey RRC configurations for LTM. The `LTM Configuration IE` in the `UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE` message contains generated lower layer RRC configurations, including LTM CSI reporting configuration(s) in the `CellGroupConfig IE`.
